mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates strengthening residential demand, which boosts construction sector revenues and financial stability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or show negative correlation with SPX in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher short-term equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of cases during mid-cycle expansion, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5% annually triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 2.5% YoY while FEDFUNDS remains < 0.5%
failure_condition: High inflation periods consistently generate positive 60-day SPX returns, or the threshold signal shows Rank IC < 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent job creation and declining unemployment confirm genuine labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or shows negative predictive power during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which directly supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or produces negative returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and potential supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Falling industrial production coincides with positive 20-day SPX returns in > 65% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) during slowdown regimes.
